title: Beauty And The Bug

jokes.text: you're no doubt curious as to why I tricked you all into hang gliding here. Close-up. It seems someone in this room is a murderer.
jokes.setup: Calculon appears to be in a murder mystery scene with audience thinking about the murder
jokes.punchline: He casually reveals he tricked them into hang gliding to get there
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur about hang gliding mixed with murder mystery tropes. Decent misdirection but fairly standard Calculon hammy acting.

jokes.text: It's me. But the real question is, which of you is the victim? And that secret I shall take to my grave.
jokes.setup: Murder mystery continues with dramatic revelation
jokes.punchline: He admits he's the murderer but then dramatically says he'll take the victim's identity to his grave
jokes.explanation: Classic Calculon melodrama with illogical reasoning. Fits character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Now, that's acting. Calculon really Shatnered the hell out of that scene.
jokes.setup: Calculon just performed an over-the-top dramatic scene
jokes.punchline: Bender uses 'Shatnered' as a verb meaning overacted dramatically
jokes.explanation: Clever verb-ification of William Shatner's name to describe overacting. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Hey, anyone want to go to the aquarium? They got a new non-biting otter.
jokes.setup: Normal invitation to aquarium
jokes.punchline: The selling point is that the otter doesn't bite, implying others do
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about future zoo safety standards. Simple but fits the world-building.

jokes.text: Wow. Aquariums have really changed since I was a boy and no one ever took me to them.
jokes.setup: Fry commenting on how aquariums have changed
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals his sad childhood of never being taken to aquariums
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment - casual reference to his neglected childhood. Well-integrated character comedy.

jokes.text: There isn't any. The water's held in place by gravity fields so there's nothing for kids to smear yogurt on.
jokes.setup: Fry asks where the glass is in the futuristic aquarium
jokes.punchline: Practical benefit is no surface for kids to smear yogurt on
jokes.explanation: Realistic parental concern about kids making messes, good observational humor about aquarium experience.

jokes.text: I wouldn't, but I'm smart. Ahhh! Help. Police. Neat.
jokes.setup: Professor warns against touching the water, Fry presumably gets attacked by sea life
jokes.punchline: Bender's completely casual 'Neat' reaction to Fry's distress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - complete indifference to others' suffering expressed economically.

jokes.text: Hang on, I need a tripod. Let me just extend the old third leg.
jokes.setup: Bender needs a tripod for his camera
jokes.punchline: He extends his literal third leg as a tripod, with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Double entendre works on literal robot level and sexual innuendo level. Decent but relies on easy innuendo.

jokes.text: Ooh, yeah. That's stable, baby.
jokes.setup: Bender extending his leg as tripod
jokes.punchline: His unnecessarily sexual commentary about stability
jokes.explanation: Extends the previous innuendo but adds little. Bender-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: They Live lens? Eh... So-so. Twilight Zone one-minute-in-the-future lens?
jokes.setup: Bender choosing different camera lenses
jokes.punchline: Absurd sci-fi movie reference lenses with casual dismissal of They Live
jokes.explanation: Creative sci-fi movie reference humor. Good for fans who get the references.

jokes.text: Film? Who uses film? We've had digital cameras for a thousand years.
jokes.setup: Bender runs out of film
jokes.punchline: In the year 3000, film photography is absurdly outdated
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor about technological advancement. Straightforward but effective.

jokes.text: No digital camera can capture the warmth and grain of good old film. How can you even tell? Your eyes are digital cameras.
jokes.setup: Bender defends film photography with artistic reasoning
jokes.punchline: Leela points out his eyes are literally digital cameras
jokes.explanation: Excellent logical contradiction humor. Perfect robot-specific punchline that undercuts photography pretension.

jokes.text: You're looking at the last chemical darkroom in existence.
jokes.setup: Someone asks where you can develop film in the year 3000
jokes.punchline: Bender happens to have the last darkroom in existence
jokes.explanation: Convenient coincidence humor. Fits Bender's character as a collector of obsolete things.

jokes.text: Yo, Calky! I'm a big fan, and I've got a big camera.
jokes.setup: Bender approaches Calculon for a photo
jokes.punchline: Big fan/big camera parallel phrasing
jokes.explanation: Simple parallel structure wordplay. Economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Try one where you're pretending to swing a tennis racquet.
jokes.setup: Bender takes photos of Calculon
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and random pose request
jokes.explanation: Random = funny approach. Mildly amusing but lazy writing technique.

jokes.text: Over here, Calculon! Come on, smile. Quit blinking. Red eye. Blurry. Lens cap's still on.
jokes.setup: Bender taking photos of rare fish
jokes.punchline: Escalating photography failures culminating in lens cap still being on
jokes.explanation: Good escalating incompetence sequence. Classic photography failure tropes but well-executed.

jokes.text: At last, we can enjoy our studio-mandated quality time.
jokes.setup: Calculon finally escapes the photographer
jokes.punchline: The 'quality time' with his TV family is corporate-mandated
jokes.explanation: Good satire of celebrity culture and corporate control. Fits Calculon's pompous character.

jokes.text: Argh! I can't not work like this. I'll be in my day-off trailer.
jokes.setup: Calculon trying to enjoy mandated time off
jokes.punchline: He has a special trailer specifically for his days off
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific absurdity. Perfect for Calculon's over-the-top celebrity lifestyle.

jokes.text: And I took this one inside the ichthyosaur's stomach. Man, I've never had anyone try so hard to digest me.
jokes.setup: Bender showing photos from inside a sea monster
jokes.punchline: Casual complaint about the creature's digestion effort
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of absurd situation. Bender treating being eaten as mild inconvenience.

jokes.text: Yeah, but a true photo buff wouldn't even use these for toilet paper. Not if curtains were available.
jokes.setup: Bender criticizing his own Calculon photos
jokes.punchline: Wouldn't use them as toilet paper, but only if curtains are available
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of self-deprecation with absurd qualifier about curtains being preferable.

jokes.text: Us People Magazine? That's total trash. Total celebrity trash. My inquiring organ wants to know.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg criticizes tabloid magazine
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits his curiosity, using 'inquiring organ' instead of 'inquiring mind'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg character moment - moral judgment followed by immediate contradiction. Good alien anatomy wordplay.

jokes.text: I have no time for such foolishness.
jokes.setup: Dismissing suggestion to go to Hollywood
jokes.punchline: Sets up immediate scene transition contradiction
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate contradiction - pure Bender character consistency

jokes.text: Well, I changed my mind and came to Hollywood after all.
jokes.setup: Previously claimed to have no time for Hollywood foolishness
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by showing up in Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender hypocrisy. Economically shows his self-serving nature and lack of consistency.

jokes.text: In fact, you might just have what it takes to be a paparazzo. Namely, a camera.
jokes.setup: Editor building up Bender's potential as a paparazzo
jokes.punchline: The only qualification is owning a camera
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of professional mystique. Perfect setup/punchline structure with economic delivery.

jokes.text: Wait, a what's-a-whatzo? A paparazzo. You know, a celebrity photographer.
jokes.setup: Bender doesn't understand the word paparazzo
jokes.punchline: He mispronounces it as 'what's-a-whatzo'
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation humor. Fits Bender's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hmm, I don't know. How much will it cost me? You misunderstand, Bender. We'll pay you.
jokes.setup: Bender expressing reluctance about becoming a paparazzo
jokes.punchline: He thinks he'll have to pay for the job opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Bender's confusion about employment. Character-appropriate misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Sir, you just hired yourself a new what's-a-whatzo.
jokes.setup: Bender accepting the paparazzo job
jokes.punchline: Still uses his mispronunciation from earlier
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to his earlier mispronunciation. Consistent character behavior but minimal humor.

jokes.text: Nice to meet you. And even though it's only my first day, I want you all to know that I'm already better than you. Stay out of my way!
jokes.setup: Bender being introduced to his new coworkers
jokes.punchline: Immediately declares superiority and makes threats on his first day
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender arrogance and antisocial behavior. Perfect character integration with good escalation.

jokes.text: Only a documentary about the greatest actor in the world. Ah, yes, Calculon. No, not that scene-chewing ham.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg watching documentary about greatest actor, Bender assumes it's Calculon
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg dismisses Calculon as a 'scene-chewing ham'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and theater terminology. 'Scene-chewing ham' is a perfect description of Calculon.

jokes.text: He's raked in seven Academy Awards for best actor and two for best actress, all without ever removing his trademark bag.
jokes.setup: Describing Langdon Cobb's acting achievements
jokes.punchline: He won best actress awards as a male actor, all while wearing a bag
jokes.explanation: Absurd achievement that defies gender categories. Good world-building for the character's mystique.

jokes.text: Langdon Cobb's acting is always in the bag.
jokes.setup: Concluding segment about Langdon Cobb
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'in the bag' meaning certain success and his literal bag
jokes.explanation: Standard pun based on his signature bag. Economical but predictable wordplay.

jokes.text: Once again, television has given me a reason to live.
jokes.setup: Bender watching the documentary about Langdon Cobb
jokes.punchline: Television provides his existential purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment about his shallow nature and dependence on entertainment.

jokes.text: One 60-foot ladder, please.
jokes.setup: Learning Cobb has a 50-foot force field around his house
jokes.punchline: Bender's simple solution is a slightly taller ladder
jokes.explanation: Excellent logical simplicity that cuts through sci-fi complications. Very economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What are you, some kind of attack fungus? Or just a moldy schnauzer?
jokes.setup: Bender encountering Cobb's guard dog/fungus creature
jokes.punchline: Can't tell if it's a weapon or just a moldy dog
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the ambiguous nature of the creature. Decent setup/punchline structure.

jokes.text: Oh, God, please let this tacky Hollywood mansion have the obligatory... Yes! Tuscan-inspired pergola!
jokes.setup: Bender praying for mansion clichés to help his escape
jokes.punchline: Specific architectural pretension pays off
jokes.explanation: Great satire of Hollywood mansion clichés. Meta-awareness of architectural pretensions in celebrity homes.

jokes.text: Peace out, Portobello.
jokes.setup: Bender escaping from the mushroom creature
jokes.punchline: Portobello mushroom reference as parting insult
jokes.explanation: Clever mushroom pun combined with casual departure. Economical and character-appropriate bravado.

jokes.text: Wait. Say that again, but say, 'Tremendous consequences.'
jokes.setup: Cobb warning about terrible consequences of showing the photo
jokes.punchline: Bender wants him to use 'tremendous' instead for better effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - more interested in dramatic presentation than actual danger. Great character priorities.

jokes.text: I swear on my honor as a paparazzo.
jokes.setup: Bender promising not to show the photo
jokes.punchline: Swears on the honor of a notoriously dishonorable profession
jokes.explanation: Good ironic oath. Paparazzi have no honor by definition, making this promise meaningless.

jokes.text: Hmm... He looked at the photo and that happened. But it could just be a coincidence.
jokes.setup: Fry becomes a deflated husk after seeing the photo
jokes.punchline: Bender considers dramatic transformation might be coincidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect robotic logical processing that ignores obvious cause and effect. Great Bender obliviousness.

jokes.text: The evidence is mounting. Although two times hardly establishes a pattern. I'd feel more comfortable raising the alarm if I were a little more certain.
jokes.setup: Amy also becomes a husk after seeing the photo
jokes.punchline: Bender still needs more evidence despite clear pattern
jokes.explanation: Continued robotic over-analysis of obvious situation. Good escalation of his scientific detachment.

jokes.text: Hey, how'd those two get deflated? They looked at this.
jokes.setup: Hermes notices the deflated crew members
jokes.punchline: Casual explanation while presumably showing the photo
jokes.explanation: Sets up the next victim through casual conversation. Economical setup for visual gag.

jokes.text: That proves it. Looking at this photo of Langdon Cobb has a horrific effect on living creatures.
jokes.setup: Three people have been deflated by the photo
jokes.punchline: Bender finally accepts the obvious conclusion
jokes.explanation: Payoff to his over-cautious scientific method. Reasonable conclusion to the pattern.

jokes.text: Hey, Zoidberg, look at this photo of Langdon Cobb. What, a picture of Langdon Cobb? Now, this I want to see as clearly as possible.
jokes.setup: Bender knowing the photo is dangerous
jokes.punchline: He deliberately shows it to Zoidberg, who enthusiastically wants to see it clearly
jokes.explanation: Dark humor of Bender's cruelty and Zoidberg's enthusiasm for his own doom. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: They're attention parasites. They feed on the admiration of their prey. Just like the noble buffalo.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining quantum lichen people's feeding habits
jokes.punchline: Compares them to buffalo, which don't feed on admiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - scientific explanation with completely inappropriate animal comparison. Memorable absurdity.

jokes.text: Nothing like a buffalo.
jokes.setup: Professor's absurd buffalo comparison
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Maximum economy and effectiveness in pointing out the absurdity.

jokes.text: So the picture robbed them of their souls? Souls? Don't be ridiculous. It's their life forces that have been stolen. It's scientific.
jokes.setup: Leela asking about souls being stolen
jokes.punchline: Professor dismisses souls as ridiculous but accepts 'life forces' as scientific
jokes.explanation: Great satire of scientific materialism's arbitrary distinctions. Perfect Professor character moment about semantics.

jokes.text: That's why the robot's immune. He has no soul. Life force!
jokes.setup: Explaining Bender's immunity to the photo
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says 'soul' then quickly corrects to 'life force'
jokes.explanation: Great callback to his semantic correction. Shows his inconsistency while maintaining the scientific facade.

jokes.text: If we poke a hole in Cobb's bag, will they spew out like a volcano built on an Indian graveyard?
jokes.setup: Asking how to return the stolen life forces
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific horror movie reference for the mechanism
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference humor mixing supernatural and geological elements. Memorably specific.

jokes.text: The ego and the id are connected not physically, like decent, God-fearing Earth lichens, but by quantum entanglement.
jokes.setup: Explaining the alien lichen's unusual biology
jokes.punchline: Describes Earth lichens as 'decent, God-fearing' organisms
jokes.explanation: Excellent anthropomorphization of lichens with moral judgments. Creative and memorable scientific explanation.

jokes.text: Let's just give up and go to the aquarium again.
jokes.setup: Facing the impossible task of finding the ego
jokes.punchline: Fry's solution is to give up and return to leisure activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry response to adversity - immediate retreat to simple pleasures. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: All right, let's go. It'll be just like stomping a puppy.
jokes.setup: Plan to subdue the actor's ego (which looks like a dog)
jokes.punchline: Bender's enthusiastic comparison to animal cruelty
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender darkness and casual cruelty. Economical expression of his sociopathic tendencies.

jokes.text: Look, I own words that are helpful.
jokes.setup: Trying to contribute to the plan
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing suggesting he 'owns words' instead of having knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg speech pattern - owns words rather than knows information

jokes.text: Quiet, Zoidberg. God, I wish you were a husk.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg contributing helpful information
jokes.punchline: Professor wishes he was drained of life force for speaking
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor crankiness toward Zoidberg. Dark wish delivered with characteristic irritation.

jokes.text: Lo, the worm has turned.
jokes.setup: Bender asking for Calculon's help
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic way to say the tables have turned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Calculon pompousness using archaic expression. Character-appropriate dramatic language.

jokes.text: He beats me out for every award, every role, every paternity suit.
jokes.setup: Listing things Cobb beats him at
jokes.punchline: Including paternity suits in list of competitions
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation ending with unexpected personal legal troubles as competition

jokes.text: That's Romeo and Juliet, dummy, by Will.i.am Shakespeare.
jokes.setup: Correcting Calculon about the play's title
jokes.punchline: Attributes it to 'Will.i.am Shakespeare' mixing the rapper's name
jokes.explanation: Clever anachronistic mixing of Will.i.am and William Shakespeare. Good wordplay but somewhat forced.

jokes.text: Food coloring? The most poisonous substance known to robots?
jokes.setup: Professor mentions the fake poison is food coloring
jokes.punchline: Food coloring is extremely toxic to robots
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor about robot physiology. Logical that artificial coloring would harm artificial beings.

jokes.text: I must perform the ultimate death scene. I shall drink the actual poison and actually die.
jokes.setup: Wanting to give best performance
jokes.punchline: Deciding actual death is the only way to achieve ultimate acting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Calculon commitment to melodrama - taking method acting to fatal extreme

jokes.text: But, Calculon, you can't kill yourself. I'm a celebrity. I can kill anyone I want.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to talk Calculon out of suicide
jokes.punchline: Calculon's twisted logic about celebrity privilege extending to murder
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark satire of celebrity culture and privilege. Perfect Calculon narcissism and twisted logic.

jokes.text: I always come in stinky.
jokes.setup: Leela calling 'Stinky' over radio
jokes.punchline: Bender's response has sexual innuendo about 'coming in stinky'
jokes.explanation: Simple innuendo based on radio code name. Economical but relies on easy sexual humor.

jokes.text: Whoof! I beg to differ.
jokes.setup: Presumably someone's body odor
jokes.punchline: Bender's reaction to actual stinkiness
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction to smell. Economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's going totally Kanye.
jokes.setup: Describing Cobb's ego inflating from attention
jokes.punchline: Uses Kanye West as reference for out-of-control ego
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference for describing ego inflation. Economical but relies on celebrity reference.

jokes.text: There's a humungous fungus among us.
jokes.setup: Observing the growing ego fungus
jokes.punchline: Rhyming play on 'huge fungus among us'
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyming wordplay. Economical but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: At least he died knowing I was great.
jokes.setup: Calculon has just died performing
jokes.punchline: Bender makes it about himself and his photography
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender narcissism in the face of tragedy. Economical expression of his self-centeredness.

jokes.text: It just has to warm up for about 15 minutes.
jokes.setup: Activating the anti-fungal ray during crisis
jokes.punchline: The advanced weapon needs a comically long warm-up time
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of sci-fi weapon tropes. Professor's inventions always have practical limitations.

jokes.text: Aw! Did you miss me, pookie?
jokes.setup: Reuniting with his aggressive ego creature
jokes.punchline: Treating dangerous monster like beloved pet
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between dangerous creature and affectionate pet treatment

jokes.text: Eh... Contrast is a little off on this one.
jokes.setup: Urgently developing photos while friends are in danger
jokes.punchline: Bender critiques photo quality instead of hurrying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priorities - artistic perfectionism over friends' lives. Good tension with the urgency.

jokes.text: This one is really good. But I can still do better.
jokes.setup: Still developing photos during crisis
jokes.punchline: Continues perfectionism despite increasing urgency
jokes.explanation: Escalation of his perfectionism. Character-consistent but less impactful than previous version.

jokes.text: Oh, God, just kill us already.
jokes.setup: Cobb explaining his process and philosophy at length
jokes.punchline: Leela preferring death to more exposition
jokes.explanation: Great reaction to villain monologue trope. Economical expression of audience frustration with exposition.

jokes.text: Oh, God, just kill us already.
jokes.setup: Cobb's pretentious monologue about 'the process'
jokes.punchline: Preferring death to more actor talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect audience surrogate reaction to pretentious actor monologue - death preferable

jokes.text: Oops! Nonetheless, I finally nailed it.
jokes.setup: Accidentally showing photo to wrong target
jokes.punchline: Pride in photographic achievement despite tactical failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priority confusion - artistic success over mission success

jokes.text: It doesn't work that way, you imbecile. Although it's really quite a nice portrait. The contrast is exquisite. And the composition sublime.
jokes.setup: Bender showing Cobb his own photo expecting it to drain him
jokes.punchline: Cobb critiques the photo's artistic merits instead
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expected outcome. Cobb's artistic appreciation mirrors Bender's earlier perfectionism.

jokes.text: I look spectacular. Aw, man. My great photo only made his ego bigger.
jokes.setup: Plan to defeat Cobb by showing him his photo
jokes.punchline: Cobb's narcissistic reaction makes him stronger instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal. The plan backfires due to Cobb's vanity, strengthening rather than weakening him.

jokes.text: I got to admit, this time, it'll be a pleasure to be in your picture.
jokes.setup: Bender wanting to take a commemorative photo after saving everyone
jokes.punchline: Leela admits it's finally a pleasure, contrasting with earlier annoyance
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment and callback to the episode's beginning setup.

jokes.text: Say, 'Bender is great.' Bender is great! Neat.
jokes.setup: Group photo after saving the day
jokes.punchline: Bender makes them say he's great, then gives his signature 'Neat' response
jokes.explanation: Nice bookend with his earlier casual 'Neat' and characteristic ego-stroking. Good episode closure.

tmdb_overview: Bender becomes a matador in the sport of "Bug Fighting."
